title: uniprot-mcp: A Model Context Protocol server for the UniProt protein knowledgebase
description:
uniprot-mcp is a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that exposes the UniProt protein knowledgebase to LLM-driven agents such as Claude. It ships 38 read-only tools across eight families: search and lookup; structural cross-references (PDB, AlphaFold per-residue pLDDT bands); variants and disease (ClinVar bridge, position-aware variant lookup, OMIM); position-aware features (active sites, signal peptides, modified residues, natural variants); composition dossier (sequence chemistry, drug-target context, disease associations); orthology; a local provenance cache with replay; and per-query provenance verification.

Every response carries a verifiable provenance record - the UniProt release number, release date, retrieval timestamp, resolved URL, and a release-aware SHA-256 digest of the canonical response bytes - so any answer can be re-verified against a current UniProt response or replayed offline from cache.

The PyPI distribution is published as uniprot-mcp-server; the installed console script and MCP server identity are both uniprot-mcp. The package is Apache-2.0 licensed and ships with a pre-registered SHA-256-committed benchmark, SLSA build provenance, Sigstore signing, and a CycloneDX SBOM for supply-chain auditability.
references: The UniProt Consortium. UniProt: the Universal Protein Knowledgebase in 2025. Nucleic Acids Research (2025). doi:10.1093/nar/gkae1010
notes: Provenance and reproducibility primitives - release-pinning, canonical SHA-256 hashing, per-query verification, local replay cache - make every tool call independently auditable years after capture. See docs/provenance-guide.md in the repository for details.
